title: armeria alpina subsp. halleri
intro: Armeria alpina subsp. halleri, also known as Alpine Sea Thrift or Alpine Thrift, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Plumbaginaceae, characterized by its small, pink or white flowers and compact, clump-forming growth habit. The plant is used in traditional herbal medicine due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which make it useful for treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Armeria alpina subsp. halleri include its ability to reduce inflammation, soothe digestive issues, and act as an expectorant to relieve coughs and congestion. Armeria alpina subsp. halleri can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
